# Slidelang

**Decks as code, generated by Claude, edited in your browser.**

You describe a deck in plain English. An AI generates a typed JSON spec. A pure compiler turns that spec into editable, presentable slides — with charts, math, code, and images — that you can review in Monaco, present in full screen, or share with a URL.

🔗 **Live:** [slidelang.vercel.app](https://slidelang.vercel.app)
🎥 **Demo (3:45):** [loom.com/share/57ccc887c6b3492d85b909e68f4124ea](https://www.loom.com/share/57ccc887c6b3492d85b909e68f4124ea)
📖 **PRD:** [`docs/PRD.md`](docs/PRD.md) · **TDD:** [`docs/TDD.md`](docs/TDD.md) · **Build notes:** [`docs/BUILD_NOTES.md`](docs/BUILD_NOTES.md)

---

## What makes it different

Most AI-slide tools either give you a fixed visual canvas you can't programmatically edit (Gamma, Beautiful.AI), or they give you raw prompt-to-HTML output that's brittle and unstructured. Slidelang takes a third path: **the AI produces a typed deck spec, not pixels and not HTML.**

The spec is the contract. Validation runs against the spec. Editing happens on the spec. The compiler is a pure function from spec to HTML — same input, same output. That makes the output trustworthy in two ways:

1. **Editable** — you change the JSON, the preview updates instantly. It's not a one-shot generation.
2. **Reliable** — bounded validation rules (e.g. "no slide has 2+ charts," "no text+math overflow") emit stable error codes. A bounded repair loop feeds those codes back to Claude to fix. At most 2 retries.

---

## Quick tour

```
You: "3 slides explaining vector embeddings with the cosine similarity formula"

→ Claude returns a typed JSON Deck (6 block types, 5 layouts)
→ Validation pipeline checks layout/density/overflow rules
→ If rules fail, Claude is asked to fix the specific issues (max 2 retries)
→ Compiler renders the validated deck to reveal.js HTML
→ Monaco editor + live preview let you tweak the JSON; preview re-renders
→ Present mode is full-screen reveal.js; arrow keys to navigate, Q to exit
```

The amber `repaired` badge on the editor page tells you when the validation pipeline triggered a fix. The green `⚡ 12.4s` badge shows generation time.

---

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────┐    POST /api/generate    ┌──────────────────────┐
│  Next.js client │ ───────────────────────► │  FastAPI backend     │
│  (Vercel)       │                          │  (Railway)           │
│                 │                          │                      │
│  - Landing      │                          │  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  - Monaco edit  │ ◄─── deck JSON + meta ── │  │  Anthropic     │  │
│  - Live preview │                          │  │  Claude        │  │
│  - Present mode │                          │  └────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────┘                          │          │           │
                                             │          ▼           │
                                             │  ┌────────────────┐  │
                                             │  │ Pydantic deck  │  │
                                             │  │ schema +       │  │
                                             │  │ validators     │  │
                                             │  └────────────────┘  │
                                             │          │           │
                                             │          ▼           │
                                             │  ┌────────────────┐  │
                                             │  │ Repair loop    │  │
                                             │  │ (max 2 retry)  │  │
                                             │  └────────────────┘  │
                                             │          │           │
                                             │          ▼           │
                                             │  ┌────────────────┐  │
                                             │  │ Pure compiler  │  │
                                             │  │ (Jinja2 →      │  │
                                             │  │  reveal.js)    │  │
                                             │  └────────────────┘  │
                                             └──────────────────────┘
```

---

## Repo layout

```
slidelang/
├── backend/                    # FastAPI + Python 3.12
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── schema/deck.py      # The DSL: typed discriminated union
│   │   ├── compiler/           # Pure deck → HTML compiler
│   │   ├── validation/         # Semantic rules + bounded repair loop
│   │   ├── ai/                 # Anthropic client, prompts, fence stripping
│   │   ├── routes/             # /generate, /compile, /decks/{id}
│   │   └── storage/            # In-memory deck store (Redis swappable)
│   └── tests/                  # 22 pytest unit tests
└── frontend/                   # Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind
    ├── app/                    # App Router pages
    │   ├── page.tsx            # Landing + prompt
    │   └── deck/[id]/          # Editor + present mode
    └── e2e/                    # 3 Playwright smoke tests
```

---

## Run it locally

Requires Python 3.12, Node 20, an Anthropic API key.

```bash
# Backend
cd backend
python3.12 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .
cp .env.example .env  # then add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000

# Frontend (separate terminal)
cd frontend
npm install
echo 'NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000' > .env.local
npm run dev
# open http://localhost:3000
```

---

## Testing

**Backend — 22 pytest tests, all critical paths covered.**

```bash
cd backend && pytest -v
```

Covers: schema validation, compiler determinism, XSS escaping (in blocks and meta), all 5 layouts, every semantic rule, repair loop convergence, repair loop exhaustion.

**Frontend — 3 Playwright smoke tests gated in CI.**

```bash
cd frontend && npm run test:e2e
```

Covers: landing renders, generation flow with timer badge, present mode with reveal.js. Runs on every push to `main` against the live Vercel URL.

Comprehensive frontend coverage is deferred to v1 — the v0 frontend is mostly orchestration over the heavily-tested backend.

---

## Tech stack

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Backend framework | FastAPI | Async, type-checked, great Pydantic integration |
| Schema | Pydantic 2 discriminated union | Tight type contracts, free JSON Schema export |
| Templates | Jinja2 | Deterministic, well-understood, autoescape on |
| AI | Claude Sonnet 4.6 via Anthropic SDK | Strong structured-output reliability |
| Frontend | Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind | Standard, well-supported |
| Editor | Monaco | Same editor as VS Code; battle-tested |
| Slide rendering | Reveal.js + KaTeX + Chart.js | All loaded from CDN, mature, presentation-quality |
| Hosting | Railway (API) + Vercel (UI) | Free tier covers v0; one-click GitHub deploys |
| CI | GitHub Actions | Backend pytest + frontend Playwright on every push |

---

## What's deferred to v1

Explicit roadmap items, not oversights:

- Comprehensive frontend E2E coverage (currently smoke tests only)
- Anthropic provider abstraction (currently single-provider; OpenRouter / Gemini are obvious next)
- Redis-backed deck storage (currently in-memory, decks disappear on container restart)
- Image generation pipeline — image blocks render fine but Claude isn't actively prompted to use them yet
- CLI tool for headless deck generation
- PPTX / PDF export
- Visual regression tests
- Cross-browser test coverage (chromium only in v0)

See [`docs/BUILD_NOTES.md`](docs/BUILD_NOTES.md) for the full build story — including five bugs found and fixed, and how I debugged them.
